description: Epic 1 tracer-bullet acceptance contract. Defines the SHAPE Orion's PR against revelara-ai/microservices-demo must satisfy. Shape-based, not content-bullseye: a 5-commit PR addressing 5 real gaps is success, not failure-because-not-3.
spec_refs: docs/SPEC/Orion-SPEC.md §12.7 Operating Envelope Reporting and Reproduction Bundle

pr.author: orion[bot]
pr.author_note: GitHub App slug. Override via $ORION_APP_NAME if the production App is named differently.

commits.min_note: Honest minimum. Original spec said 'exactly 3' against a contrived fixture; against the real microservices-demo, Orion may legitimately ship 1, 5, or N PRs depending on detected gaps and what passes the verifier.

pr.must_modify_note: microservices-demo's services live under src/. Any reliability fix must touch a file under that tree. Helm charts, manifests, and protos are out of scope for v1 reliability synthesis.

body.must_contain_note: Markdown headings or labeled sections. The verifier produces these; PR composer interpolates them per SPEC §16.1.

reset.procedure: Manual runbook documented in docs/fixtures/README.md. Operator confirms no in-flight non-Orion PRs, then runs `git fetch upstream && git checkout main && git reset --hard upstream/main && git push --force-with-lease origin main`. A turnkey script is deferred until the safety guards have been exercised manually enough times to trust automation.
reset.upstream_protection: The upstream remote (GoogleCloudPlatform/microservices-demo) is read-only. NO Orion path (PRs, pushes, gh ops) may target upstream. epic1_smoke.sh refuses to operate against any owner in the forbidden list (GoogleCloudPlatform, googlecloudplatform); exit code 20 is the safety violation. The reset runbook reinforces this constraint in prose.
